metadata.from: Bob (Robert) Hanifen — signed 'Love, Bob' on final page, writing from University of Wyoming, Laramie
metadata.envelope_notes: No envelope image provided. Letter written on University of Wyoming 'Cowboy Joe' letterhead.
metadata.mood_tone: Cheerful, upbeat, and detail-oriented; Bob is eager to reassure his father about finances and enjoys recounting social activities and physical training experiences.
metadata.legibility_notes: Handwriting is generally readable but cursive is rushed in places; some words require guessing (e.g., 'toilet article' area, some proper names). Several words are crossed out or overwritten. 'Jesse Cook' is a best guess for one girl's name.
metadata.historical_context: Letter appears to be from a military training program context (possibly ROTC or early WWII-era military training), referencing physical training, obstacle courses, typhoid shots, room inspections, passes, and confinement to campus. References to Fort Leavenworth suggest prior military posting. Frontier Days in Cheyenne is an annual rodeo event.
metadata.date_estimation_reasoning: Multi-day letter (Monday to Friday) from University of Wyoming, Laramie on 'Cowboy Joe' letterhead. References Frontier Days in Cheyenne (annual rodeo, last week of July), a September 30–October 12 leave, fishing with Alvin (who appears in Letter 31a from June 1945 in Laramie), physical training, and Pauline/John/Fran. The Frontier Days reference places the start of the letter around late July 1944. The mention of snow starting around September 16 and invitation to come before August for fishing aligns with summer 1944. The leave dates of Sept. 30–Oct. 12 are almost identical to Letter 14's leave of Sept. 30–Oct. 10, confirming the same period. Estimated August 1944.

pages.transcription:
Mon.

Dear Dad, We have a free period so I thought I would write you a letter. The expense account is coming along all right. I have 16 dollars left & I loaned Jim 6 dollars in Fort Leavenworth. I had to get my blouse tailored and it took 2.25 for that, 1.43 for towels & wash rags, a couple bucks for [that?] toilet articles. I have only spent a 1.60 on girls, I let them take me out, or just go for a walk. I do my own laundry like my socks & under wear, I have to send my other clothes to the laundry. It takes about 30¢ a week for it. I spent about 3.00 in Leavenworth and about 2.22 in Denver. Now that we have got settled I only spend about 1.00 a week. We get a leave the last of [the?] 30th of Sept. to Oct. 12. If you want to come up than would be a time; come up about a couple days before my leave and I could ride home with you. That other money went to

something, but I don't for what. I am taking it pretty easy now and I am going to make up for the loss. Every Sunday they want to go on a trip to the mountain. It costs about $2.00. I haven't yet, because this Sunday we are going down to some kid's [or?] uncle's house for the weekend. Next week we are going to hitchhike to Cheyenne to see the Frontier Days. 1 dollar a week would get me along fine. Everything thing in this town is pretty reasonable except haircuts they cost 75¢. I don't know where I could get you a fish pole to use. Some kid here got to go fishing with some men Sunday and they caught about 6 trout apiece. I have gained 4 pounds now. The P.T. is really making me rugged, a 35 min. of exercises, 1 hr. 30 of football, the obstacle courses, and a half mile. All the other guys have spent a lot more money than I have & I move so fast. They want about a buck for a good meal in town. I haven't started way of it yet, but I come awful close to it. I am going to try [to?] to find Alvin again, maybe I can get a couple of meals.

Friday       Dear Dad

I will start writing again, it is the first opportunity I have had since Mon. It is

2.

about 20:06 now. We just got another typhoid shot, what a blast, I can hardly write, got a bump as big as an egg. A lot of things have happened since Mon. I met Alvin and am going fishing with him Sun. He says for you to come up he has a couple extra poles. The fishing is really good he say. It starts snowing about Sept. 16 so he say. If you want to fish or even get here you had better come around in August. I spent the buck Wed. night. My Bud's boy friend who is a acting second [lieutenant?] came up and got a 3 hr. pass till 10:30. Had a pretty good [time?]. The girls I have been going is a thing of the past. I dropped her & found two others. One's name is Marian Devant she lives in Laramie, & the other Jesse Cook lives about 22 miles out of town. She drives in and we have a pretty good time. Got a letter from Jim & John,

They are really bearing down on us now. If the rooms don't pass inspection & you with, it no pass for the week — you are confined to the campus. I done my school work & [also?] made my bed and feel pretty good but not too good. I now weigh a pound & a half more since Mon. Tell Fran & Pauline hello & John too. Have really been busy. Always on the ball. Got a bugler now who tries to beat the gong every morn. There are looking for him & when we do, [waiting?] for him. Tell Kay hello and thanks for letter. Tell the boys hello too. Tell Les his crate would blow up in this altitude. We went on a picnic in the mountains Sun. up to the summit, the highest [point?] on the Lincoln Highway, 8360. The 38 Chev. darn near blew up. Every mile or so it ran out water. We had to walk the last 6 miles to the top. We could see 6 states from a lookout station. Some ladies took my picture up there & am sending them as soon as I can.

Well good by for now
Love, Bob

full_transcription:
Mon.

Dear Dad, We have a free period so I thought I would write you a letter. The expense account is coming along all right. I have 16 dollars left & I loaned Jim 6 dollars in Fort Leavenworth. I had to get my blouse tailored and it took 2.25 for that, 1.43 for towels & wash rags, a couple bucks for [that?] toilet articles. I have only spent a 1.60 on girls, I let them take me out, or just go for a walk. I do my own laundry like my socks & under wear, I have to send my other clothes to the laundry. It takes about 30¢ a week for it. I spent about 3.00 in Leavenworth and about 2.22 in Denver. Now that we have got settled I only spend about 1.00 a week. We get a leave the last of [the?] 30th of Sept. to Oct. 12. If you want to come up than would be a time; come up about a couple days before my leave and I could ride home with you. That other money went to

something, but I don't for what. I am taking it pretty easy now and I am going to make up for the loss. Every Sunday they want to go on a trip to the mountain. It costs about $2.00. I haven't yet, because this Sunday we are going down to some kid's [or?] uncle's house for the weekend. Next week we are going to hitchhike to Cheyenne to see the Frontier Days. 1 dollar a week would get me along fine. Everything thing in this town is pretty reasonable except haircuts they cost 75¢. I don't know where I could get you a fish pole to use. Some kid here got to go fishing with some men Sunday and they caught about 6 trout apiece. I have gained 4 pounds now. The P.T. is really making me rugged, a 35 min. of exercises, 1 hr. 30 of football, the obstacle courses, and a half mile. All the other guys have spent a lot more money than I have & I move so fast. They want about a buck for a good meal in town. I haven't started way of it yet, but I come awful close to it. I am going to try [to?] to find Alvin again, maybe I can get a couple of meals.

Friday       Dear Dad

I will start writing again, it is the first opportunity I have had since Mon. It is

about 20:06 now. We just got another typhoid shot, what a blast, I can hardly write, got a bump as big as an egg. A lot of things have happened since Mon. I met Alvin and am going fishing with him Sun. He says for you to come up he has a couple extra poles. The fishing is really good he say. It starts snowing about Sept. 16 so he say. If you want to fish or even get here you had better come around in August. I spent the buck Wed. night. My Bud's boy friend who is a acting second [lieutenant?] came up and got a 3 hr. pass till 10:30. Had a pretty good [time?]. The girls I have been going is a thing of the past. I dropped her & found two others. One's name is Marian Devant she lives in Laramie, & the other Jesse Cook lives about 22 miles out of town. She drives in and we have a pretty good time. Got a letter from Jim & John,

They are really bearing down on us now. If the rooms don't pass inspection & you with, it no pass for the week — you are confined to the campus. I done my school work & [also?] made my bed and feel pretty good but not too good. I now weigh a pound & a half more since Mon. Tell Fran & Pauline hello & John too. Have really been busy. Always on the ball. Got a bugler now who tries to beat the gong every morn. There are looking for him & when we do, [waiting?] for him. Tell Kay hello and thanks for letter. Tell the boys hello too. Tell Les his crate would blow up in this altitude. We went on a picnic in the mountains Sun. up to the summit, the highest [point?] on the Lincoln Highway, 8360. The 38 Chev. darn near blew up. Every mile or so it ran out water. We had to walk the last 6 miles to the top. We could see 6 states from a lookout station. Some ladies took my picture up there & am sending them as soon as I can.

Well good by for now
Love, Bob
summary: This is a multi-day letter (started Monday, continued Friday) from Bob Hanifen to his father, written on University of Wyoming letterhead while Bob is undergoing military training in Laramie. Bob provides a detailed account of his expenses, reassuring his father he is managing money carefully, doing his own laundry, and avoiding unnecessary spending. He describes vigorous physical training (PT, obstacle courses, football), socializing with girls, plans to see Frontier Days in Cheyenne, a painful typhoid shot, and an exciting mountain hike up to the summit of the Lincoln Highway at 8,360 feet where they could see 6 states. He also mentions meeting Alvin (who has fishing poles and invites Dad to visit), receiving a letter from Jim and John, and the strict military discipline including campus confinement for failed room inspections.
